triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git detection, artifact root exclusion, and comprehensive test coverage.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function implements file discovery with git ls-files fallback to directory walk
evidence.why: This is the core boundary snapshot implementation that handles both git and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: gitFiles function uses git ls-files with -co flags for tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: This implements git-backed file discovery for the boundary snapshot.

evidence.summary: walkFiles function provides fallback file discovery for non-git projects
evidence.why: This ensures boundary detection works even without git repository.

evidence.summary: Allowed write roots exclusion logic filters out artifact directories from boundary checks
evidence.why: This implements the artifact root exclusion requirement.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots function compares before/after states to detect mutations
evidence.why: This provides the actual boundary violation detection logic.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime builds boundary snapshots before/after agent execution and reports violations
evidence.why: This shows the complete boundary audit workflow in production.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations create BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force prd_ready=false
evidence.why: This demonstrates the enforcement consequence of boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test verifies untracked file mutation detection
evidence.why: This confirms the boundary detection works with git repositories.

evidence.summary: Non-git boundary test verifies file walk fallback and artifact exclusion
evidence.why: This confirms the boundary detection works without git and excludes artifact roots.

evidence.summary: General boundary blocking test confirms agent mutations are detected even with passing results
evidence.why: This verifies boundary enforcement takes priority over agent success status.

evidence.summary: Integration test runs full explorer→verifier pipeline and confirms boundary-clean artifacts
evidence.why: This shows the complete evidence dispatch workflow with successful boundary enforcement.

evidence.summary: Execution policy defines boundary restrictions and allowed write roots in dispatch plan
evidence.why: This documents the boundary contract presented to evidence agents.

risks: Full-tool agent could theoretically bypass boundary if file system write tools are unrestricted
Edge cases like git worktrees or submodules might not be covered by current git ls-files approach

result.notes: Implementation exists with comprehensive test coverage. Boundary snapshot uses git ls-files with fallback walk, excludes artifact roots, and enforces violations with blockers. Ready for stress testing with real Claude -p agents.

agent_results.claim: Verified: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git detection, artifact root exclusion, and comprehensive test coverage.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function implements dual-mode file discovery: git ls-files with fallback to recursive directory walk
evidence.why: Primary evidence for git-backed and non-git project support.

evidence.summary: Artifact root exclusion via allowedWriteRoots filtering: files in artifact roots are omitted from boundary snapshots
evidence.why: Verifies the dispatch artifact root exclusion requirement.

evidence.summary: Test suite covers git-backed (untracked file detection) and non-git (directory walk + artifact exclusion) scenarios
evidence.why: Confirms comprehensive test coverage for both detection modes.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations result in BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker code and force prd_ready=false
evidence.why: Verifies enforcement mechanism for boundary violations.

evidence.summary: Dispatch artifact root directory exists at expected path
evidence.why: Confirms artifact root structure for exclusion logic.

risks: Explorer evidence reports full implementation, but stress-testing with real Claude -p agents in dirty working trees has not been verified
missing: Stress test evidence from real Claude -p agent runs in dirty working trees
Production usage logs showing boundary detection performance under load

result.notes: Explorer evidence is accurate and sufficient for PRD readiness. All 12 evidence records reference actual project files with correct line numbers. No assumptions promoted to facts. Missing items are operational validation gaps, not implementation gaps. The acceptance criteria for git-backed and non-git detection plus artifact exclusion are met by verified implementation.
